Fix a wall-clock-timing-fragile asyncio test that fails reproducibly on slow CI runners. The test uses a fixed real-time await asyncio.sleep(0.18) with a 0.05s scheduler tick and asserts an AsyncMock call_count==2 (initial send plus one retry); on a loaded 2-core CI runner the margin is unreliable and task.cancel() races synchronous code, so the retry tick is cancelled and call_count is 1. Make the timing-dependent scheduler tests deterministic (wait on a synchronization primitive for exactly N sends instead of a fixed sleep) without weakening the assertions. Several sibling tests in the same file share the 0.18s/0.05s pattern. Python test debugging, must remain green under load and any test ordering.
